title: T-013 ‚Äî Reference table seeds + GDPR erasure + i18n PL/EN keys (MNT ¬ß13.2, ¬ß14.4, ¬ß14.5)
prompt:
# T-013 ‚Äî Reference tables + GDPR + i18n bootstrap

PRD: docs/prd/13-MAINTENANCE-PRD.md ¬ß13.2 (maintenance_alert_thresholds, technician_skills), ¬ß14.4 (GDPR), ¬ß14.5 (i18n PL/EN)
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Three co-ordinated deliverables: (a) seed Settings reference tables `maintenance_alert_thresholds` + `technician_skills` per ¬ß13.2; (b) extend Foundation `app.gdpr_erase_user(user_id)` (Wave1 T-114) to pseudonymise `technician_profiles` PII per ¬ß14.4; (c) emit PL/EN i18n keys under `maintenance.*` namespace per ¬ß14.5 for all error codes from T-010/T-011/T-015/T-016 and screen titles in MNT-015..035.

## Pre-flight
Verify Wave1 T-113/T-114 GDPR primitive. Verify Foundation T-022 i18n / next-intl scaffold. Verify 02-Settings ¬ß8.1 reference tables registry pattern.

## Implementation contract
1. Seed migration `packages/db/migrations/NNNN_maintenance_settings_seed.sql`:
   - INSERT into `reference_tables_registry` two entries: `maintenance_alert_thresholds`, `technician_skills` (per 02-Settings pattern).
   - INSERT default values for Apex baseline: `maintenance_alert_thresholds (pm_interval_default_days=30, calibration_warning_days=30, calibration_urgent_days=7, availability_breach_threshold_pct=80.00, atp_rlu_threshold=30, mtbf_target_threshold_pct=NULL)`.
   - INSERT 3 technician_skills rows: basic, advanced, specialist.
2. Extend `apps/web/lib/gdpr/erase-user.ts` (Wave1 T-114) with a maintenance-specific function `eraseMaintenanceUser(userId)` that:
   - Pseudonymises `technician_profiles`: certifications‚ÜíNULL, hourly_rate‚ÜíNULL, user_id‚Üíhash(user_id, salt) per ¬ß14.4 GDPR-vs-regulatory conflict resolution (prefer pseudonymise over delete to preserve 7y audit).
   - Preserves `mwo.requester_user_id`/`mwo.approver_user_id`/`mwo.assigned_to_user_id` references but updates them to the hashed ID (so audit chain holds).
   - Returns count of pseudonymised rows.
   - Registers itself with Foundation GDPR orchestrator so `app.gdpr_erase_user()` picks it up.
3. i18n: create `apps/web/messages/maintenance.pl.json` + `apps/web/messages/maintenance.en.json` with these namespaces (verify by importing in vitest):
   - `maintenance.errors.MWO_INVALID_TRANSITION`, `‚Ä¶APPROVER_SAME_AS_REQUESTER`, `‚Ä¶LOTO_NOT_VERIFIED`, `‚Ä¶SANITATION_DUAL_SIGNOFF_REQUIRED`, `‚Ä¶SPARE_INSUFFICIENT_STOCK`, etc.
   - `maintenance.assets.list.*`, `maintenance.mwo.detail.*`, `maintenance.pm.list.*`, `maintenance.calibration.list.*`, `maintenance.spares.list.*`, `maintenance.loto.list.*`, `maintenance.settings.*` (UI surfaces from MNT-015..035).
4. Vitest: seed runs idempotently; gdpr fn pseudonymises technician_profiles correctly; PL/EN files parse + every error code from T-010/T-011 has a translation key in both files.

## Files
- packages/db/migrations/NNNN_maintenance_settings_seed.sql [create]
- apps/web/lib/gdpr/maintenance.ts [create]
- apps/web/lib/gdpr/__tests__/maintenance.test.ts [create]
- apps/web/messages/maintenance.pl.json [create]
- apps/web/messages/maintenance.en.json [create]
- apps/web/messages/__tests__/maintenance-i18n.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given seed migration runs, when SELECT FROM maintenance_alert_thresholds runs, then exactly one row with Apex baseline values is present per ¬ß13.2.
2. Given seed re-runs, when checked, then no error (ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING) and no row duplication.
3. Given a user_id with 2 technician_profiles rows, when eraseMaintenanceUser(user_id) runs, then certifications=NULL, hourly_rate=NULL, user_id=hash; mwo references to that user are also hashed; count returned matches rows touched.
4. Given the i18n PL/EN files load, when imported in vitest, then every error code raised by T-010/T-011 Server Actions has a key in both `maintenance.pl.json` and `maintenance.en.json`.

## Test strategy
- RED: three vitest files asserting all four ACs.
- Run `pnpm --filter db test maintenance-settings-seed && pnpm --filter @monopilot/web test gdpr/maintenance && pnpm --filter @monopilot/web test maintenance-i18n`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not DELETE technician_profiles rows ‚Äî ¬ß14.4 mandates pseudonymise over delete to preserve regulatory 7y audit trail.
- Do not skip GDPR registration with Foundation orchestrator ‚Äî would break Foundation success criterion ¬ß13.
- Do not omit error-code keys in i18n ‚Äî translation gap is a release-blocker for UI.
- Do not seed for tenants other than 'default' ‚Äî that is a tenant-onboarding Settings task.

pipeline_inputs.description: Seed maintenance_alert_thresholds + technician_skills + extend GDPR erase + emit PL/EN i18n keys for all error codes + screen titles.
pipeline_inputs.details: Closes Auditor A cross-cutting gaps: GDPR erasure (¬ß13 Foundation success criterion) and i18n PL/EN (¬ß14.5 BRCGS audit reqt). Pseudonymise per ¬ß14.4 (prefer over delete for 7y retention). LOTO and calibration sign-off error keys (LOTO_PIN_INVALID, CAL_CERT_IMMUTABLE etc.) in i18n files require @monopilot/e-sign foundation primitive (T-124) for PIN verification ‚Äî signEvent/dualSign with replay nonce + paired audit (retention='security').

Given i18n PL/EN files load, when imported in vitest, then every error code from T-010/T-011 has a key in both files
